Cows take up so much room, and they make so much poop. It's bad for the soil, and it's bad for the air. We should just eat plants instead. They're good for us, and they don't hurt the earth as much. ALUs, plants are yummy! Have you tried tofu? It's so good! And you can put it in anything. My favorite is tofu stir fry with broccoli and carrots. It's so good!

I understand you are thinking about implementing a policy that would require students to maintain at least a B average to participate in sports or other activities. I believe allowing students with a 2.5 GPA to engage in sports would be fair, as not everyone excels academically, yet many shine in athletics.

I propose evaluating students based on group performance. For instance, you could administer a test in a particular subject and then calculate an average score from the results. This approach ensures everyone has a fair chance. If some students still struggle, they should be encouraged to work harder. They can seek help after school, perhaps through tutoring or studying with resources, to improve their grades.

Once students improve and make the team, they would practice daily, applying the skills they've learned. After practice, they could continue their studies, preparing for important tests. Achieving at least a C average should be the aim since it represents average performance, while a B is above average.
